commands.review_summary:
The PR adds one draft RFC describing plugin-owned external approval verification via `requireApproval.externalResolution` command templates and an ownership-checked `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` resolver.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a design RFC PR, not a bug report. The review checked the added RFC, current main, related RFC history, GitHub review state, and the linked implementation candidate instead of reproducing a runtime failure.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer RFC, security, and product-direction review rather than an automated repair branch.

target.title: Plugin-owned external verification approvals
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a draft RFC for plugin-owned external verification of approval resolution. The proposal keeps OpenClaw core as the owner of pending approval state, routing, denial, timeout, cancellation, and final tool continuation, while giving external plugins one narrow route to run verifier-specific proof outside core.

The proposed host surface is intentionally small:

- `requireApproval.externalResolution`: one verifier label, one command template, and allow-style external decisions
- `plugin.approval.resolveVerified`: an ownership-checked resolver for the plugin that created the pending approval

## What changed after review

Updated after maintainer feedback to keep the RFC high-signal:

- rebased on latest `openclaw/rfcs` `main`
- removed PR-history narration from Motivation
- removed safer-auto-mode/blog framing from Motivation and PR notes
- removed durable status-card scope from this RFC
- clarified that auto review does not move approvals into the external-verification lane and requires no change for this boundary
- resolved the previously open decisions in the RFC body:
  - resolver authority uses `operator.approvals`; external plugin helpers must not require `operator.admin`
  - `allow-always` persistence is plugin-owned policy, not a new core auto-approval lane
  - v1 ships command templates only, not arbitrary plugin-defined actions
  - audit fields are approval metadata only; no verifier proof payloads or private IDs in core
  - channels that do not understand `externalResolution` degrade to command text

## Reference implementation

The concrete reference implementation is the AgentKit ClawHub plugin:

- https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit
- https://clawhub.ai/plugins/@guardiola31337/agentkit

The RFC now describes AgentKit only in terms of public plugin entry points and the end-user flow, so the host API stays generic and not World-specific.

## Validation

RFC branch validation:

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Reference implementation proof expected before acceptance / implementation:

- `pnpm test:hitl` in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit`
- `pnpm test:openclaw-hitl` in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit`
- short manual TUI recording or redacted log showing Verify once, Verify and trust for session, Deny, World QR/link, and resumed protected tool execution

The proof commands and manual user-flow steps are documented in the AgentKit repo; the shareable proof artifact still needs to be attached before this moves out of draft.
target.author: guardiola31337
